title.raw: 《孝景本紀》 Annals of the Xiaojing Emperor

sentences.zh: 孝景皇帝者，孝文之中子也。

translations.literal: Emperor Xiaojing was the middle son of Emperor Xiaowen.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing was the middle son of Emperor Wen.

translations.literal: His mother was Empress Dowager Dou.
translations.idiomatic: His mother was Empress Dowager Dou.

sentences.zh: 孝文在代時，前后有三男，及竇太后得幸，前后死，及三子更死，故孝景得立。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xiaowen was in Dai, he had three sons by his former queen. After Empress Dowager Dou gained his favor, the former queen died, and the three sons also died one after another. Therefore Emperor Xiaojing was able to be established.
translations.idiomatic: While Emperor Wen was still in Dai, he had three sons by his former consort. After Lady Dou won his favor, that consort died, and the three sons died in turn as well. This was how Emperor Jing came to be named heir.

translations.literal: Emperor Xiaojing was the middle son of Emperor Xiaowen. His mother was Empress Dowager Dou. When Emperor Xiaowen was in Dai, he had three sons by his former queen. After Empress Dowager Dou gained his favor, the former queen died, and the three sons also died one after another. Therefore Emperor Xiaojing was able to be established.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing was the middle son of Emperor Wen. His mother was Empress Dowager Dou. While Emperor Wen was still in Dai, he had three sons by his former consort. After Lady Dou won his favor, that consort died, and the three sons died in turn as well. This was how Emperor Jing came to be named heir.

translations.literal: In the fourth month of the first year, on the yimao day, he granted amnesty to all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of his first year, on the yimao day, he granted a general amnesty throughout the empire.

translations.literal: On the yisi day, he bestowed one grade of noble rank on the people.
translations.idiomatic: On the yisi day, he granted the common people one grade of noble rank.

sentences.zh: 五月，除田半租，為孝文立太宗廟。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, he abolished the half land tax and established the Taizong temple for Emperor Xiaowen.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month, he abolished the half-tax on agricultural land and established the Taizong temple for Emperor Wen.

translations.literal: He ordered the assembled ministers not to offer court congratulations.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the ministers to dispense with the court congratulations ceremony.

translations.literal: The Xiongnu entered Dai, and he made a treaty of heqin with them.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu invaded Dai, and he concluded a heqin peace treaty with them.

translations.literal: In the fourth month of the first year, on the yimao day, he granted amnesty to all under Heaven. On the yisi day, he bestowed one grade of noble rank on the people. In the fifth month, he abolished the half land tax and established the Taizong temple for Emperor Xiaowen. He ordered the assembled ministers not to offer court congratulations. The Xiongnu entered Dai, and he made a treaty of heqin with them.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of his first year, on the yimao day, he granted a general amnesty throughout the empire. On the yisi day, he granted the common people one grade of noble rank. In the fifth month, he abolished the half-tax on agricultural land and established the Taizong temple for Emperor Wen. He ordered the ministers to dispense with the court congratulations ceremony. The Xiongnu invaded Dai, and he concluded a heqin peace treaty with them.

sentences.zh: 二年春，封故相國蕭何孫系為武陵侯。

translations.literal: In spring of the second year, he enfeoffed Xi, grandson of the former Chancellor Xiao He, as Marquis of Wuling.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of his second year, he enfeoffed Xi, grandson of the former Chancellor Xiao He, as Marquis of Wuling.

translations.literal: Males received service registration at twenty.
translations.idiomatic: Men became liable for service registration at age twenty.

translations.literal: In the fourth month, on the renwu day, Empress Dowager Xiaowen died.
translations.idiomatic: On the renwu day of the fourth month, Empress Dowager Xiaowen passed away.

translations.literal: The Kings of Guangchuan and Changsha both went to their states.
translations.idiomatic: The Kings of Guangchuan and Changsha both went to their fiefs.

translations.literal: Chancellor Shentu Jia died.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Shentu Jia passed away.

sentences.zh: 八月，以御史大夫開封陶青為丞相。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, he made Tao Qing of Kaifeng, the Grandee Secretary, Chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month, he appointed Tao Qing of Kaifeng, the Grandee Secretary, as Chancellor.

translations.literal: A comet appeared in the northeast.
translations.idiomatic: A comet appeared in the northeast.

sentences.zh: 秋，衡山雨雹，大者五寸，深者二尺。

translations.literal: In autumn, hail fell in Hengshan; the largest stones were five cun, and the deepest accumulation was two chi.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, hail fell in Hengshan, with the largest stones five cun across and accumulations two chi deep.

translations.literal: Mars moved retrograde and guarded Beichen.
translations.idiomatic: Mars moved in retrograde motion and lingered near Beichen.

translations.literal: The moon appeared among Beichen.
translations.idiomatic: The moon appeared near Beichen.

translations.literal: Jupiter moved retrograde within the Heavenly Court.
translations.idiomatic: Jupiter moved in retrograde motion within the Heavenly Court constellation.

sentences.zh: 置南陵及內史、祋祤為縣。

translations.literal: He established Nanling, and also Neishi and Duiyu, as counties.
translations.idiomatic: He established Nanling, Neishi, and Duiyu as counties.

translations.literal: In spring of the second year, he enfeoffed Xi, grandson of the former Chancellor Xiao He, as Marquis of Wuling. Males received service registration at twenty. In the fourth month, on the renwu day, Empress Dowager Xiaowen died. The Kings of Guangchuan and Changsha both went to their states. Chancellor Shentu Jia died. In the eighth month, he made Tao Qing of Kaifeng, the Grandee Secretary, Chancellor. A comet appeared in the northeast. In autumn, hail fell in Hengshan; the largest stones were five cun, and the deepest accumulation was two chi. Mars moved retrograde and guarded Beichen. The moon appeared among Beichen. Jupiter moved retrograde within the Heavenly Court. He established Nanling, and also Neishi and Duiyu, as counties.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of his second year, he enfeoffed Xi, grandson of the former Chancellor Xiao He, as Marquis of Wuling. Men became liable for service registration at age twenty. On the renwu day of the fourth month, Empress Dowager Xiaowen passed away. The Kings of Guangchuan and Changsha both went to their fiefs. Chancellor Shentu Jia passed away. In the eighth month, he appointed Tao Qing of Kaifeng, the Grandee Secretary, as Chancellor. A comet appeared in the northeast. In autumn, hail fell in Hengshan, with the largest stones five cun across and accumulations two chi deep. Mars moved in retrograde motion and lingered near Beichen. The moon appeared near Beichen. Jupiter moved in retrograde motion within the Heavenly Court constellation. He established Nanling, Neishi, and Duiyu as counties.

translations.literal: In the first month of the third year, on the yisi day, he granted amnesty to all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of his third year, on the yisi day, he granted a general amnesty throughout the empire.

translations.literal: A long star appeared in the west.
translations.idiomatic: A comet appeared in the west.

sentences.zh: 天火燔雒陽東宮大殿城室。

translations.literal: Heavenly fire burned the great hall and buildings of the Eastern Palace at Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: Heavenly fire burned the great hall and buildings of the Eastern Palace at Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 吳王濞、楚王戊、趙王遂、膠西王卬、濟南王辟光、菑川王賢、膠東王雄渠反，發兵西鄉。

translations.literal: King Bi of Wu, King Wu of Chu, King Sui of Zhao, King Ang of Jiaoxi, King Piguang of Jinan, King Xian of Zichuan, and King Xiongqu of Jiaodong rebelled, raised troops, and faced west.
translations.idiomatic: The Kings Bi of Wu, Wu of Chu, Sui of Zhao, Ang of Jiaoxi, Piguang of Jinan, Xian of Zichuan, and Xiongqu of Jiaodong rebelled, raised armies, and marched west.

sentences.zh: 天子為誅晁錯，遣袁盎諭告，不止，遂西圍梁。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven executed Chao Cuo for their sake and sent Yuan Ang to explain and admonish them, but they did not stop, and then went west to besiege Liang.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor executed Chao Cuo on their account and sent Yuan Ang to explain his decision and admonish them. They still did not stop, and went west to besiege Liang.

sentences.zh: 上乃遣大將軍竇嬰、太尉周亞夫將兵誅之。

translations.literal: The emperor then sent Grand General Dou Ying and Grand Commandant Zhou Yafu to lead troops and punish them.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then sent Grand General Dou Ying and Grand Commandant Zhou Yafu to lead troops and suppress the rebellion.

translations.literal: In the sixth month, on the yihai day.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month, on the yihai day.

sentences.zh: 赦亡軍及楚元王子藝等與謀反者。

translations.literal: He granted amnesty to deserters and to Yi, son of King Yuan of Chu, and the others who had conspired in rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: He pardoned deserters, along with Yi, son of King Yuan of Chu, and others who had taken part in the conspiracy.

sentences.zh: 封大將軍竇嬰為魏其侯。

translations.literal: Enfeoffed Grand General Dou Ying as Marquis of Weiqi.
translations.idiomatic: He enfeoffed Grand General Dou Ying as the Marquis of Weiqi.

sentences.zh: 立楚元王子平陸侯禮為楚王。

translations.literal: He established Li, son of King Yuan of Chu and Marquis of Pinglu, as King of Chu.
translations.idiomatic: He installed Li, son of King Yuan of Chu and Marquis of Pinglu, as King of Chu.

sentences.zh: 立皇子端為膠西王，子勝為中山王。

translations.literal: Installed imperial son Duan as the King of Jiaoxi, son Sheng as the King of Zhongshan.
translations.idiomatic: He installed his son Duan as the King of Jiaoxi and his son Sheng as the King of Zhongshan.

sentences.zh: 徙濟北王志為菑川王，淮陽王餘為魯王，汝南王非為江都王。

translations.literal: He transferred King Zhi of Jibei to be King of Zichuan, King Yu of Huaiyang to be King of Lu, and King Fei of Runan to be King of Jiangdu.
translations.idiomatic: He transferred King Zhi of Jibei to Zichuan, King Yu of Huaiyang to Lu, and King Fei of Runan to Jiangdu.

translations.literal: King Jianglu of Qi and King Jia of Yan both died.
translations.idiomatic: King Jianglu of Qi and King Jia of Yan both passed away.

translations.literal: In the first month of the third year, on the yisi day, he granted amnesty to all under Heaven. A long star appeared in the west. Heavenly fire burned the great hall and buildings of the Eastern Palace at Luoyang. King Bi of Wu, King Wu of Chu, King Sui of Zhao, King Ang of Jiaoxi, King Piguang of Jinan, King Xian of Zichuan, and King Xiongqu of Jiaodong rebelled, raised troops, and faced west. The Son of Heaven executed Chao Cuo for their sake and sent Yuan Ang to explain and admonish them, but they did not stop, and then went west to besiege Liang. The emperor then sent Grand General Dou Ying and Grand Commandant Zhou Yafu to lead troops and punish them. In the sixth month, on the yihai day. He granted amnesty to deserters and to Yi, son of King Yuan of Chu, and the others who had conspired in rebellion. Enfeoffed Grand General Dou Ying as Marquis of Weiqi. He established Li, son of King Yuan of Chu and Marquis of Pinglu, as King of Chu. Installed imperial son Duan as the King of Jiaoxi, son Sheng as the King of Zhongshan. He transferred King Zhi of Jibei to be King of Zichuan, King Yu of Huaiyang to be King of Lu, and King Fei of Runan to be King of Jiangdu. King Jianglu of Qi and King Jia of Yan both died.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of his third year, on the yisi day, he granted a general amnesty throughout the empire. A comet appeared in the west. Heavenly fire burned the great hall and buildings of the Eastern Palace at Luoyang. The Kings Bi of Wu, Wu of Chu, Sui of Zhao, Ang of Jiaoxi, Piguang of Jinan, Xian of Zichuan, and Xiongqu of Jiaodong rebelled, raised armies, and marched west. The emperor executed Chao Cuo on their account and sent Yuan Ang to explain his decision and admonish them. They still did not stop, and went west to besiege Liang. The emperor then sent Grand General Dou Ying and Grand Commandant Zhou Yafu to lead troops and suppress the rebellion. In the sixth month, on the yihai day. He pardoned deserters, along with Yi, son of King Yuan of Chu, and others who had taken part in the conspiracy. He enfeoffed Grand General Dou Ying as the Marquis of Weiqi. He installed Li, son of King Yuan of Chu and Marquis of Pinglu, as King of Chu. He installed his son Duan as the King of Jiaoxi and his son Sheng as the King of Zhongshan. He transferred King Zhi of Jibei to Zichuan, King Yu of Huaiyang to Lu, and King Fei of Runan to Jiangdu. King Jianglu of Qi and King Jia of Yan both passed away.

translations.literal: Fourth year summer, established crown prince.
translations.idiomatic: In the summer of his fourth year, he established the crown prince.

translations.literal: Installed imperial son Che as King of Jiaodong.
translations.idiomatic: He installed his son Che as King of Jiaodong.

translations.literal: Sixth month jiaxu, granted amnesty to all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiaxu day of the sixth month, he granted a general amnesty throughout the empire.

sentences.zh: 後九月，更以（弋）［易］陽為陽陵。

translations.literal: In the intercalary ninth month, he changed Yiyang to Yangling.
translations.idiomatic: In the intercalary ninth month, he renamed Yiyang as Yangling.

translations.literal: He reestablished the ferry checkpoints and used travel passes for entry and exit.
translations.idiomatic: He reestablished the ferry checkpoints and required travel passes for entry and exit.

translations.literal: Winter, made Zhao state into Handan commandery.
translations.idiomatic: In winter, he converted the state of Zhao into Handan commandery.

translations.literal: Fourth year summer, established crown prince. Installed imperial son Che as King of Jiaodong. Sixth month jiaxu, granted amnesty to all under Heaven. In the intercalary ninth month, he changed Yiyang to Yangling. He reestablished the ferry checkpoints and used travel passes for entry and exit. Winter, made Zhao state into Handan commandery.
translations.idiomatic: In the summer of his fourth year, he established the crown prince. He installed his son Che as King of Jiaodong. On the jiaxu day of the sixth month, he granted a general amnesty throughout the empire. In the intercalary ninth month, he renamed Yiyang as Yangling. He reestablished the ferry checkpoints and required travel passes for entry and exit. In winter, he converted the state of Zhao into Handan commandery.

translations.literal: In the third month of the fifth year, he built Yangling and the Wei Bridge.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month of his fifth year, he built Yangling and the Wei Bridge.

sentences.zh: 五月，募徙陽陵，予錢二十萬。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, he recruited people to move to Yangling and gave them 200,000 cash.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month, he recruited settlers for Yangling and gave them 200,000 cash.

sentences.zh: 江都大暴風從西方來，壞城十二丈。

translations.literal: At Jiangdu, a great violent wind came from the west and damaged twelve zhang of the city wall.
translations.idiomatic: At Jiangdu, a great gale came from the west and damaged twelve zhang of the city wall.

sentences.zh: 丁卯，封長公主子蟜為隆慮侯。

translations.literal: On the dingmao day, he enfeoffed Qiao, son of the eldest princess, as Marquis of Longlu.
translations.idiomatic: On the dingmao day, he enfeoffed Qiao, son of the eldest princess, as Marquis of Longlu.

translations.literal: Transferred King of Guangchuan to be King of Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: He transferred the King of Guangchuan to become King of Zhao.

translations.literal: In the third month of the fifth year, he built Yangling and the Wei Bridge. In the fifth month, he recruited people to move to Yangling and gave them 200,000 cash. At Jiangdu, a great violent wind came from the west and damaged twelve zhang of the city wall. On the dingmao day, he enfeoffed Qiao, son of the eldest princess, as Marquis of Longlu. Transferred King of Guangchuan to be King of Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month of his fifth year, he built Yangling and the Wei Bridge. In the fifth month, he recruited settlers for Yangling and gave them 200,000 cash. At Jiangdu, a great gale came from the west and damaged twelve zhang of the city wall. On the dingmao day, he enfeoffed Qiao, son of the eldest princess, as Marquis of Longlu. He transferred the King of Guangchuan to become King of Zhao.

sentences.zh: 六年春，封中尉（趙）綰為建陵侯，江都丞相嘉為建平侯，隴西太守渾邪為平曲侯，趙丞相嘉為江陵侯，故將軍布為鄃侯。

translations.literal: In spring of the sixth year, he enfeoffed Commandant Zhao Wan as Marquis of Jianling, Jia the Chancellor of Jiangdu as Marquis of Jianping, Hunye the Governor of Longxi as Marquis of Pingqu, Jia the Chancellor of Zhao as Marquis of Jiangling, and Bu the former general as Marquis of Shu.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of his sixth year, he enfeoffed Zhao Wan, the Commandant, as Marquis of Jianling; Jia, Chancellor of Jiangdu, as Marquis of Jianping; Hunye, Governor of Longxi, as Marquis of Pingqu; Jia, Chancellor of Zhao, as Marquis of Jiangling; and Bu, a former general, as Marquis of Shu.

translations.literal: Liang Chu two kings both passed away.
translations.idiomatic: The Kings of Liang and Chu both passed away.

sentences.zh: 後九月，伐馳道樹，殖蘭池。

translations.literal: In the intercalary ninth month, he cut down trees along the imperial highways and planted them by Orchid Pond.
translations.idiomatic: In the intercalary ninth month, he had trees cut down along the imperial highways and replanted by Orchid Pond.

translations.literal: In spring of the sixth year, he enfeoffed Commandant Zhao Wan as Marquis of Jianling, Jia the Chancellor of Jiangdu as Marquis of Jianping, Hunye the Governor of Longxi as Marquis of Pingqu, Jia the Chancellor of Zhao as Marquis of Jiangling, and Bu the former general as Marquis of Shu. Liang Chu two kings both passed away. In the intercalary ninth month, he cut down trees along the imperial highways and planted them by Orchid Pond.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of his sixth year, he enfeoffed Zhao Wan, the Commandant, as Marquis of Jianling; Jia, Chancellor of Jiangdu, as Marquis of Jianping; Hunye, Governor of Longxi, as Marquis of Pingqu; Jia, Chancellor of Zhao, as Marquis of Jiangling; and Bu, a former general, as Marquis of Shu. The Kings of Liang and Chu both passed away. In the intercalary ninth month, he had trees cut down along the imperial highways and replanted by Orchid Pond.

sentences.zh: 七年冬，廢栗太子為臨江王。

translations.literal: In winter of the seventh year, he deposed Crown Prince Li and made him King of Linjiang.
translations.idiomatic: In the winter of his seventh year, he deposed Crown Prince Li and made him King of Linjiang.

translations.literal: The eleventh month last day, the sun was eclipsed.
translations.idiomatic: On the last day of the eleventh month, there was a solar eclipse.

translations.literal: In spring, he pardoned the convicts and bondservants working on Yangling.
translations.idiomatic: In spring, he pardoned the convicts and bondservants working on the Yangling tomb.

translations.literal: Chancellor Qing was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Qing was dismissed from office.

sentences.zh: 二月乙巳，以太尉條侯周亞夫為丞相。

translations.literal: In the second month, on the yisi day, he made Zhou Yafu, Grand Commandant and Marquis of Tiao, Chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: On the yisi day of the second month, he appointed Zhou Yafu, Grand Commandant and Marquis of Tiao, as Chancellor.

sentences.zh: 四月乙巳，立膠東王太后為皇后。

translations.literal: In the fourth month, on the yisi day, he established the mother of the King of Jiaodong as Empress.
translations.idiomatic: On the yisi day of the fourth month, he established the mother of the King of Jiaodong as Empress.

translations.literal: On the dingsi day, he established the King of Jiaodong as crown prince.
translations.idiomatic: On the dingsi day, he established the King of Jiaodong as crown prince.

translations.literal: Named Che.
translations.idiomatic: His name was Che.

translations.literal: In winter of the seventh year, he deposed Crown Prince Li and made him King of Linjiang. The eleventh month last day, the sun was eclipsed. In spring, he pardoned the convicts and bondservants working on Yangling. Chancellor Qing was dismissed. In the second month, on the yisi day, he made Zhou Yafu, Grand Commandant and Marquis of Tiao, Chancellor. In the fourth month, on the yisi day, he established the mother of the King of Jiaodong as Empress. On the dingsi day, he established the King of Jiaodong as crown prince. Named Che.
translations.idiomatic: In the winter of his seventh year, he deposed Crown Prince Li and made him King of Linjiang. On the last day of the eleventh month, there was a solar eclipse. In spring, he pardoned the convicts and bondservants working on the Yangling tomb. Chancellor Qing was dismissed from office. On the yisi day of the second month, he appointed Zhou Yafu, Grand Commandant and Marquis of Tiao, as Chancellor. On the yisi day of the fourth month, he established the mother of the King of Jiaodong as Empress. On the dingsi day, he established the King of Jiaodong as crown prince. His name was Che.

sentences.zh: 中元年，封故御史大夫周苛孫平為繩侯，故御史大夫周昌（子）［孫］左車為安陽侯，四月乙巳，赦天下，賜爵一級。

translations.literal: In the first year of the middle period, he enfeoffed Ping, grandson of the former Grandee Secretary Zhou Ke, as Marquis of Sheng, and Zuoju, grandson of the former Grandee Secretary Zhou Chang, as Marquis of Anyang. In the fourth month, on the yisi day, he granted amnesty to all under Heaven and bestowed one grade of noble rank.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of the middle period, he enfeoffed Ping, grandson of the former Grandee Secretary Zhou Ke, as Marquis of Sheng, and Zuoju, grandson of the former Grandee Secretary Zhou Chang, as Marquis of Anyang. On the yisi day of the fourth month, he granted a general amnesty throughout the empire and bestowed one grade of noble rank.

translations.literal: He abolished legal disabilities.
translations.idiomatic: He lifted the restrictions imposed by legal disabilities.

translations.literal: Earth moved.
translations.idiomatic: There was an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 衡山、原都雨雹，大者尺八寸。

translations.literal: Hail fell at Hengshan and Yuandu, with the largest stones measuring one chi and eight cun.
translations.idiomatic: Hail fell at Hengshan and Yuandu, with the largest stones measuring one chi and eight cun across.

translations.literal: In the first year of the middle period, he enfeoffed Ping, grandson of the former Grandee Secretary Zhou Ke, as Marquis of Sheng, and Zuoju, grandson of the former Grandee Secretary Zhou Chang, as Marquis of Anyang. In the fourth month, on the yisi day, he granted amnesty to all under Heaven and bestowed one grade of noble rank. He abolished legal disabilities. Earth moved. Hail fell at Hengshan and Yuandu, with the largest stones measuring one chi and eight cun.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of the middle period, he enfeoffed Ping, grandson of the former Grandee Secretary Zhou Ke, as Marquis of Sheng, and Zuoju, grandson of the former Grandee Secretary Zhou Chang, as Marquis of Anyang. On the yisi day of the fourth month, he granted a general amnesty throughout the empire and bestowed one grade of noble rank. He lifted the restrictions imposed by legal disabilities. There was an earthquake. Hail fell at Hengshan and Yuandu, with the largest stones measuring one chi and eight cun across.

sentences.zh: 中二年二月，匈奴入燕，遂不和親。

translations.literal: In the second month of the second year of the middle period, the Xiongnu entered Yan, and thereafter he did not maintain heqin relations.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month of the second year of the middle period, the Xiongnu invaded Yan, and he therefore abandoned heqin relations.

translations.literal: In the third month, he summoned the King of Linjiang to come.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month, he summoned the King of Linjiang to court.

translations.literal: He then died in the Commandant's office.
translations.idiomatic: He died there in the Commandant's office.

sentences.zh: 夏，立皇子越為廣川王，子寄為膠東王。

translations.literal: In summer, he established his son Yue as King of Guangchuan and his son Ji as King of Jiaodong.
translations.idiomatic: In summer, he established his son Yue as King of Guangchuan and his son Ji as King of Jiaodong.

translations.literal: He enfeoffed four marquises.
translations.idiomatic: He enfeoffed four marquises.

translations.literal: Ninth month jiaxu, solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiaxu day of the ninth month, there was a solar eclipse.

translations.literal: In the second month of the second year of the middle period, the Xiongnu entered Yan, and thereafter he did not maintain heqin relations. In the third month, he summoned the King of Linjiang to come. He then died in the Commandant's office. In summer, he established his son Yue as King of Guangchuan and his son Ji as King of Jiaodong. He enfeoffed four marquises. Ninth month jiaxu, solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month of the second year of the middle period, the Xiongnu invaded Yan, and he therefore abandoned heqin relations. In the third month, he summoned the King of Linjiang to court. He died there in the Commandant's office. In summer, he established his son Yue as King of Guangchuan and his son Ji as King of Jiaodong. He enfeoffed four marquises. On the jiaxu day of the ninth month, there was a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 中三年冬，罷諸侯御史中丞。

translations.literal: In winter of the third year of the middle period, he abolished the feudal lords' Censor-in-Chief offices.
translations.idiomatic: In the winter of the third year of the middle period, he abolished the office of Censor-in-Chief in the feudal states.

sentences.zh: 春，匈奴王二人率其徒來降，皆封為列侯。

translations.literal: In spring, two Xiongnu kings led their followers and came to surrender; both were enfeoffed as full marquises.
translations.idiomatic: In spring, two Xiongnu kings led their followers in surrendering, and both were enfeoffed as full marquises.

translations.literal: In winter of the third year of the middle period, he abolished the feudal lords' Censor-in-Chief offices. In spring, two Xiongnu kings led their followers and came to surrender; both were enfeoffed as full marquises.
translations.idiomatic: In the winter of the third year of the middle period, he abolished the office of Censor-in-Chief in the feudal states. In spring, two Xiongnu kings led their followers in surrendering, and both were enfeoffed as full marquises.

translations.literal: Middle fourth year third month, established Deyang Palace.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month of the fourth year of the middle period, he established the Deyang Palace.

translations.literal: Great locusts.
translations.idiomatic: There was a great locust plague.

translations.literal: Autumn, granted amnesty to convicts working on Yangling.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, he granted amnesty to convicts working on the Yangling tomb.

translations.literal: Middle fourth year third month, established Deyang Palace. Great locusts. Autumn, granted amnesty to convicts working on Yangling.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month of the fourth year of the middle period, he established the Deyang Palace. There was a great locust plague. In autumn, he granted amnesty to convicts working on the Yangling tomb.

sentences.zh: 中五年夏，立皇子舜為常山王。

translations.literal: Middle fifth year summer, established imperial son Shun as King of Changshan.
translations.idiomatic: In the summer of the fifth year of the middle period, he established his son Shun as King of Changshan.

translations.literal: Enfeoffed ten marquises.
translations.idiomatic: He enfeoffed ten marquises.

sentences.zh: 六月丁巳，赦天下，賜爵一級。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, on the dingsi day, he granted amnesty to all under Heaven and bestowed one grade of noble rank.
translations.idiomatic: On the dingsi day of the sixth month, he granted a general amnesty throughout the empire and bestowed one grade of noble rank.

translations.literal: All under Heaven great floods.
translations.idiomatic: There were great floods throughout the empire.

translations.literal: He changed the title of the feudal lords' chancellors to xiang.
translations.idiomatic: He changed the title of the feudal lords' chancellors, calling them simply xiang.

translations.literal: Autumn, earth moved.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, there was an earthquake.

translations.literal: Middle fifth year summer, established imperial son Shun as King of Changshan. Enfeoffed ten marquises. In the sixth month, on the dingsi day, he granted amnesty to all under Heaven and bestowed one grade of noble rank. All under Heaven great floods. He changed the title of the feudal lords' chancellors to xiang. Autumn, earth moved.
translations.idiomatic: In the summer of the fifth year of the middle period, he established his son Shun as King of Changshan. He enfeoffed ten marquises. On the dingsi day of the sixth month, he granted a general amnesty throughout the empire and bestowed one grade of noble rank. There were great floods throughout the empire. He changed the title of the feudal lords' chancellors, calling them simply xiang. In autumn, there was an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 中六年二月己卯，行幸雍，郊見五帝。

translations.literal: In the second month of the sixth year of the middle period, on the jimao day, he went in imperial progress to Yong and performed the suburban audience sacrifices to the Five Emperors.
translations.idiomatic: On the jimao day of the second month of the sixth year of the middle period, he made an imperial progress to Yong and performed the suburban sacrifices to the Five Emperors.

translations.literal: Third month, rained hail.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month, it rained hail.

sentences.zh: 四月，梁孝王、城陽共王、汝南王皆薨。

translations.literal: In the fourth month, King Xiao of Liang, King Gong of Chengyang, and the King of Runan all died.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month, King Xiao of Liang, King Gong of Chengyang, and the King of Runan all passed away.

sentences.zh: 立梁孝王子明為濟川王，子彭離為濟東王，子定為山陽王，子不識為濟陰王。

translations.literal: He established Ming, son of King Xiao of Liang, as King of Jichuan; his son Pengli as King of Jidong; his son Ding as King of Shanyang; and his son Bushi as King of Jiyin.
translations.idiomatic: He established Ming, son of King Xiao of Liang, as King of Jichuan; Pengli as King of Jidong; Ding as King of Shanyang; and Bushi as King of Jiyin.

translations.literal: Liang was divided into five.
translations.idiomatic: The kingdom of Liang was divided into five parts.

translations.literal: Enfeoffed four marquises.
translations.idiomatic: He enfeoffed four marquises.

sentences.zh: 更命廷尉為大理，將作少府為將作大匠，主爵中尉為都尉，長信詹事為長信少府，將行為大長秋，大行為行人，奉常為太常，典客為大行，治粟內史為大農。

translations.literal: He changed the title of the Commandant of Justice to Grand Judge, the Chamberlain for the Palace Buildings to Grand Artisan, the Commandant in Charge of Noble Ranks to Commandant, the Chamberlain for Changxin to Lesser Chamberlain of Changxin, the Jiangxing to Grand Changqiu, the Grand Herald to Traveller, the Fengchang to Grand Constant, the Superintendent of Guests to Grand Herald, and the Grain Intendant of the Capital to Grand Minister of Agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: He renamed several offices: the Commandant of Justice became Grand Judge; the Chamberlain for the Palace Buildings became Grand Artisan; the Commandant in Charge of Noble Ranks became Commandant; the Chamberlain for Changxin became Lesser Chamberlain of Changxin; the Jiangxing became Grand Changqiu; the Grand Herald became Traveller; the Fengchang became Grand Constant; the Superintendent of Guests became Grand Herald; and the Grain Intendant of the Capital became Grand Minister of Agriculture.

sentences.zh: 以大內為二千石，置左右內官，屬大內。

translations.literal: He made the Grand Interior a two-thousand-shi office, and established left and right interior officials subordinate to the Grand Interior.
translations.idiomatic: He made the Grand Interior a two-thousand-shi office and established left and right interior officials under it.

translations.literal: Seventh month xinhai, solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: On the xinhai day of the seventh month, there was a solar eclipse.

translations.literal: Eighth month, Xiongnu entered Shangjun.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month, the Xiongnu invaded Shangjun commandery.

translations.literal: In the second month of the sixth year of the middle period, on the jimao day, he went in imperial progress to Yong and performed the suburban audience sacrifices to the Five Emperors. Third month, rained hail. In the fourth month, King Xiao of Liang, King Gong of Chengyang, and the King of Runan all died. He established Ming, son of King Xiao of Liang, as King of Jichuan; his son Pengli as King of Jidong; his son Ding as King of Shanyang; and his son Bushi as King of Jiyin. Liang was divided into five. Enfeoffed four marquises. He changed the title of the Commandant of Justice to Grand Judge, the Chamberlain for the Palace Buildings to Grand Artisan, the Commandant in Charge of Noble Ranks to Commandant, the Chamberlain for Changxin to Lesser Chamberlain of Changxin, the Jiangxing to Grand Changqiu, the Grand Herald to Traveller, the Fengchang to Grand Constant, the Superintendent of Guests to Grand Herald, and the Grain Intendant of the Capital to Grand Minister of Agriculture. He made the Grand Interior a two-thousand-shi office, and established left and right interior officials subordinate to the Grand Interior. Seventh month xinhai, solar eclipse. Eighth month, Xiongnu entered Shangjun.
translations.idiomatic: On the jimao day of the second month of the sixth year of the middle period, he made an imperial progress to Yong and performed the suburban sacrifices to the Five Emperors. In the third month, it rained hail. In the fourth month, King Xiao of Liang, King Gong of Chengyang, and the King of Runan all passed away. He established Ming, son of King Xiao of Liang, as King of Jichuan; Pengli as King of Jidong; Ding as King of Shanyang; and Bushi as King of Jiyin. The kingdom of Liang was divided into five parts. He enfeoffed four marquises. He renamed several offices: the Commandant of Justice became Grand Judge; the Chamberlain for the Palace Buildings became Grand Artisan; the Commandant in Charge of Noble Ranks became Commandant; the Chamberlain for Changxin became Lesser Chamberlain of Changxin; the Jiangxing became Grand Changqiu; the Grand Herald became Traveller; the Fengchang became Grand Constant; the Superintendent of Guests became Grand Herald; and the Grain Intendant of the Capital became Grand Minister of Agriculture. He made the Grand Interior a two-thousand-shi office and established left and right interior officials under it. On the xinhai day of the seventh month, there was a solar eclipse. In the eighth month, the Xiongnu invaded Shangjun commandery.

sentences.zh: 後元年冬，更命中大夫令為衛尉。

translations.literal: In winter of the first year of the later period, he changed the title of Commander of the Palace Gentlemen to Commandant of the Guards.
translations.idiomatic: In the winter of the first year of the later period, he renamed the Commander of the Palace Gentlemen as Commandant of the Guards.

sentences.zh: 三月丁酉，赦天下，賜爵一級，中二千石、諸侯相爵右庶長。

translations.literal: In the third month, on the dingyou day, he granted amnesty to all under Heaven and bestowed one grade of noble rank. Officials of middle two-thousand-shi rank and feudal lords' chancellors received the noble rank of Right Shuzhang.
translations.idiomatic: On the dingyou day of the third month, he granted a general amnesty throughout the empire and bestowed one grade of noble rank. Officials of middle two-thousand-shi rank and the chancellors of feudal lords received the rank of Right Shuzhang.

translations.literal: Fourth month, great assembly.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month, there was a great public celebration with drinking.

sentences.zh: 五月丙戌，地動，其蚤食時復動。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, on the bingxu day, the earth moved, and it moved again at early-meal time.
translations.idiomatic: On the bingxu day of the fifth month, there was an earthquake, and it shook again at breakfast time.

sentences.zh: 上庸地動二十二日，壞城垣。

translations.literal: Shangyong earth moved twenty-two days, damaged city walls.
translations.idiomatic: In Shangyong, the earth shook for twenty-two days and damaged the city walls.

translations.literal: Seventh month yisi, solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: On the yisi day of the seventh month, there was a solar eclipse.

translations.literal: Chancellor Liu She dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Liu She was dismissed from office.

sentences.zh: 八月壬辰，以御史大夫綰為丞相，封建陵侯。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, on the renchen day, he made Wan, the Grandee Secretary, Chancellor and enfeoffed him as Marquis of Jianling.
translations.idiomatic: On the renchen day of the eighth month, he appointed Wan, the Grandee Secretary, as Chancellor and enfeoffed him as Marquis of Jianling.

translations.literal: In winter of the first year of the later period, he changed the title of Commander of the Palace Gentlemen to Commandant of the Guards. In the third month, on the dingyou day, he granted amnesty to all under Heaven and bestowed one grade of noble rank. Officials of middle two-thousand-shi rank and feudal lords' chancellors received the noble rank of Right Shuzhang. Fourth month, great assembly. In the fifth month, on the bingxu day, the earth moved, and it moved again at early-meal time. Shangyong earth moved twenty-two days, damaged city walls. Seventh month yisi, solar eclipse. Chancellor Liu She dismissed. In the eighth month, on the renchen day, he made Wan, the Grandee Secretary, Chancellor and enfeoffed him as Marquis of Jianling.
translations.idiomatic: In the winter of the first year of the later period, he renamed the Commander of the Palace Gentlemen as Commandant of the Guards. On the dingyou day of the third month, he granted a general amnesty throughout the empire and bestowed one grade of noble rank. Officials of middle two-thousand-shi rank and the chancellors of feudal lords received the rank of Right Shuzhang. In the fourth month, there was a great public celebration with drinking. On the bingxu day of the fifth month, there was an earthquake, and it shook again at breakfast time. In Shangyong, the earth shook for twenty-two days and damaged the city walls. On the yisi day of the seventh month, there was a solar eclipse. Chancellor Liu She was dismissed from office. On the renchen day of the eighth month, he appointed Wan, the Grandee Secretary, as Chancellor and enfeoffed him as Marquis of Jianling.

sentences.zh: 後二年正月，地一日三動。

translations.literal: In the first month of the second year of the later period, the earth moved three times in one day.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the second year of the later period, the earth shook three times in one day.

translations.literal: General Zhi attacked the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: General Zhi attacked the Xiongnu.

translations.literal: Drinking celebration five days.
translations.idiomatic: There was a public drinking celebration for five days.

sentences.zh: 令內史郡不得食馬粟，沒入縣官。

translations.literal: He ordered that the commanderies under the Interior not feed grain to horses, and that such grain be confiscated into government stores.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the Interior commanderies not to feed grain to horses; any such grain was to be confiscated for government use.

translations.literal: He ordered convicts and bondservants to wear clothing made of seven-zong cloth.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered convicts and bondservants to wear clothing made of seven-zong cloth.

translations.literal: He stopped the use of horses for pounding grain.
translations.idiomatic: He ended the practice of using horses to pound grain.

sentences.zh: 為歲不登，禁天下食不造歲。

translations.literal: Because the harvest did not mature, he forbade all under Heaven from eating grain not produced in the current year.
translations.idiomatic: Because the harvest had failed, he forbade people throughout the empire from eating grain that was not from the current year.

translations.literal: He reduced the marquises and sent them to their states.
translations.idiomatic: He reduced the number of marquises at court and sent them back to their fiefs.

translations.literal: Third month, Xiongnu entered Yanmen.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month, the Xiongnu invaded Yanmen commandery.

translations.literal: In the tenth month, he collected rent from the Changling fields.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month, he leased out the Changling fields for rent.

translations.literal: Great drought.
translations.idiomatic: There was a great drought.

sentences.zh: 衡山國、河東、雲中郡民疫。

translations.literal: The people of Hengshan state and of Hedong and Yunzhong commanderies suffered an epidemic.
translations.idiomatic: An epidemic struck the people of Hengshan state and the Hedong and Yunzhong commanderies.

translations.literal: In the first month of the second year of the later period, the earth moved three times in one day. General Zhi attacked the Xiongnu. Drinking celebration five days. He ordered that the commanderies under the Interior not feed grain to horses, and that such grain be confiscated into government stores. He ordered convicts and bondservants to wear clothing made of seven-zong cloth. He stopped the use of horses for pounding grain. Because the harvest did not mature, he forbade all under Heaven from eating grain not produced in the current year. He reduced the marquises and sent them to their states. Third month, Xiongnu entered Yanmen. In the tenth month, he collected rent from the Changling fields. Great drought. The people of Hengshan state and of Hedong and Yunzhong commanderies suffered an epidemic.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the second year of the later period, the earth shook three times in one day. General Zhi attacked the Xiongnu. There was a public drinking celebration for five days. He ordered the Interior commanderies not to feed grain to horses; any such grain was to be confiscated for government use. He ordered convicts and bondservants to wear clothing made of seven-zong cloth. He ended the practice of using horses to pound grain. Because the harvest had failed, he forbade people throughout the empire from eating grain that was not from the current year. He reduced the number of marquises at court and sent them back to their fiefs. In the third month, the Xiongnu invaded Yanmen commandery. In the tenth month, he leased out the Changling fields for rent. There was a great drought. An epidemic struck the people of Hengshan state and the Hedong and Yunzhong commanderies.

sentences.zh: 後三年十月，日月皆（食）赤五日。

translations.literal: In the tenth month of the third year of the later period, the sun and moon were both red for five days.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of the third year of the later period, both the sun and the moon appeared red for five days.

translations.literal: Twelfth month last day, thunder.
translations.idiomatic: There was thunder on the last day of the twelfth month.

translations.literal: Sun like purple.
translations.idiomatic: The sun appeared purple.

translations.literal: The five planets moved retrograde and guarded Taiwei.
translations.idiomatic: The five planets moved in retrograde motion and lingered near Taiwei.

translations.literal: The moon passed through the center of the Heavenly Court.
translations.idiomatic: The moon passed through the center of the Heavenly Court constellation.

translations.literal: In the first month, on the jiayin day, the imperial crown prince underwent the capping ceremony.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiayin day of the first month, the crown prince underwent his coming-of-age capping ceremony.

translations.literal: On the jiazi day, Emperor Xiaojing died.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiazi day, Emperor Jing passed away.

sentences.zh: 遺詔賜諸侯王以下至民為父後爵一級，天下戶百錢。

translations.literal: His testamentary edict bestowed one grade of noble rank on feudal kings and those below them, down to commoners who were heirs to their fathers, and gave one hundred cash to each household under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: In his final edict, he granted one grade of noble rank to feudal kings and everyone below them, down to commoners who inherited their fathers' households, and gave one hundred cash to every household in the empire.

sentences.zh: 出宮人歸其家，復無所與。

translations.literal: He released palace women to return to their homes, restoring the households that had no one to depend on.
translations.idiomatic: He released palace women to return home, thereby restoring households that had lacked someone to rely on.

sentences.zh: 太子即位，是為孝武皇帝。

translations.literal: The crown prince ascended the throne; this was Emperor Xiaowu.
translations.idiomatic: The crown prince ascended the throne; this was Emperor Wu.

sentences.zh: 三月，封皇太后弟蚡為武安侯，弟勝為周陽侯。

translations.literal: In the third month, he enfeoffed Fen, younger brother of the Empress Dowager, as Marquis of Wuan, and her younger brother Sheng as Marquis of Zhouyang.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month, he enfeoffed Tian Fen, the Empress Dowager's younger brother, as Marquis of Wuan, and Tian Sheng, another younger brother, as Marquis of Zhouyang.

translations.literal: He established Yangling.
translations.idiomatic: He established Yangling.

translations.literal: In the tenth month of the third year of the later period, the sun and moon were both red for five days. Twelfth month last day, thunder. Sun like purple. The five planets moved retrograde and guarded Taiwei. The moon passed through the center of the Heavenly Court. In the first month, on the jiayin day, the imperial crown prince underwent the capping ceremony. On the jiazi day, Emperor Xiaojing died. His testamentary edict bestowed one grade of noble rank on feudal kings and those below them, down to commoners who were heirs to their fathers, and gave one hundred cash to each household under Heaven. He released palace women to return to their homes, restoring the households that had no one to depend on. The crown prince ascended the throne; this was Emperor Xiaowu. In the third month, he enfeoffed Fen, younger brother of the Empress Dowager, as Marquis of Wuan, and her younger brother Sheng as Marquis of Zhouyang. He established Yangling.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of the third year of the later period, both the sun and the moon appeared red for five days. There was thunder on the last day of the twelfth month. The sun appeared purple. The five planets moved in retrograde motion and lingered near Taiwei. The moon passed through the center of the Heavenly Court constellation. On the jiayin day of the first month, the crown prince underwent his coming-of-age capping ceremony. On the jiazi day, Emperor Jing passed away. In his final edict, he granted one grade of noble rank to feudal kings and everyone below them, down to commoners who inherited their fathers' households, and gave one hundred cash to every household in the empire. He released palace women to return home, thereby restoring households that had lacked someone to rely on. The crown prince ascended the throne; this was Emperor Wu. In the third month, he enfeoffed Tian Fen, the Empress Dowager's younger brother, as Marquis of Wuan, and Tian Sheng, another younger brother, as Marquis of Zhouyang. He established Yangling.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：漢興，孝文施大德，天下懷安，至孝景，不復憂異姓，而晁錯刻削諸侯，遂使七國俱起，合從而西鄉，以諸侯太盛，而錯為之不以漸也。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian said: When the Han arose, Emperor Xiaowen practiced great virtue, and all under Heaven cherished peace. By Emperor Xiaojing, there was no longer anxiety over rulers of different surnames, but Chao Cuo sharply curtailed the feudal lords, and so caused the seven states to rise together, unite in alliance, and face west. This was because the feudal lords were too powerful, and Cuo acted without gradual steps.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian says: When the Han arose, Emperor Wen practiced great virtue, and the empire rested in peace. By Emperor Jing's time, there was no longer any fear of kings from other surnames. But Chao Cuo cut sharply into the power of the feudal lords, causing the seven states to rise together, join in alliance, and march west. The problem was that the feudal lords had grown too powerful, and Cuo did not proceed gradually.

sentences.zh: 及主父偃言之，而諸侯以弱，卒以安。

translations.literal: When Zhufu Yan spoke of it, the feudal lords were thereby weakened, and in the end there was peace.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhufu Yan later proposed his policy, the feudal lords were weakened, and the realm was finally at peace.

translations.literal: The pivot of safety and danger, is it not in planning?
translations.idiomatic: Does not the turning point between security and peril lie in policy and planning?

translations.literal: The Grand Historian said: When the Han arose, Emperor Xiaowen practiced great virtue, and all under Heaven cherished peace. By Emperor Xiaojing, there was no longer anxiety over rulers of different surnames, but Chao Cuo sharply curtailed the feudal lords, and so caused the seven states to rise together, unite in alliance, and face west. This was because the feudal lords were too powerful, and Cuo acted without gradual steps. When Zhufu Yan spoke of it, the feudal lords were thereby weakened, and in the end there was peace. The pivot of safety and danger, is it not in planning?
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian says: When the Han arose, Emperor Wen practiced great virtue, and the empire rested in peace. By Emperor Jing's time, there was no longer any fear of kings from other surnames. But Chao Cuo cut sharply into the power of the feudal lords, causing the seven states to rise together, join in alliance, and march west. The problem was that the feudal lords had grown too powerful, and Cuo did not proceed gradually. When Zhufu Yan later proposed his policy, the feudal lords were weakened, and the realm was finally at peace. Does not the turning point between security and peril lie in policy and planning?
